title: pilosella calodon
intro: Pilosella calodon, also known as the Alpine Mouse-ear or Alpine Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. Pilosella calodon can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
